This family is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world and contains over 500 species of plants. Decaloba pallida is further classified within the genus Passiflora, which includes species that are often referred to as passionflowers. The species Decaloba pallida is a member of the Passifloraceae family and is native to the Americas. It is an evergreen climber or liana, characterized by its distinctive flowers and edible fruit.
uses: Decaloba pallida is known for its use in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of fever and malaria. Its roots and leaves are often employed to create medicinal concoctions. In some regions, it is used to treat diarrhea and other gastrointestinal issues.
